name: Patrick Cordero - Abogado de Bancarrota, Miami
description_short: Miami-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, foreclosure defense, and family law with a 50+ person staff.
description_long:
The Law Offices of Patrick L. Cordero, PA is an established Miami law firm that has grown to become one of the largest consumer bankruptcy practices in South Florida. The firm was founded with a focus on helping individuals navigate financial distress resulting from illness, accidents, family issues, or other life circumstances. With over 50 staff members, the firm has expanded beyond bankruptcy to handle real estate matters including foreclosure defense, loan modifications, and closings, as well as family law practice areas.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, debt collection defense, debt settlement, and house/car protection strategies. They also provide real estate services covering foreclosure defense, short sales, and mortgage modifications. Additionally, the practice includes family law services such as divorce representation, alimony negotiations, child support, custody matters, and prenuptial agreements. The firm advertises $0 down payment options and flexible payment plans for bankruptcy clients.

The firm differentiates itself through free initial consultations, a multilingual staff that serves Spanish-speaking clients, and emphasis on straightforward communication and personalized service. Their marketing positions them as offering fast, simple, and affordable representation. The firm claims to provide honest assessments of client options and a clear game plan rather than just processing cases.

While the firm claims significant experience and scale, specific attorney credentials, years in practice for Patrick L. Cordero himself, and measurable case outcome data are not provided on the website. The website contains placeholder statistics (showing "0+" for years of experience and case counts), which undermines confidence in the stated qualifications. Clients should verify individual attorney licensing and request specific case references before engaging services.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments into one monthly bill. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments and debt management plans. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure the bankruptcy filing is accurately reported and outdated items are removed on schedule. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility during the multi-year recovery process. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan — even a small one with higher rates — can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Large staff of 50+ members suggesting capacity to handle multiple concurrent cases
Offers free initial consultations with no upfront cost required
Bilingual firm serving both English and Spanish-speaking clients
Flexible payment options available including $0 down programs
Comprehensive practice areas beyond bankruptcy including foreclosure defense and family law
Located in Miami with extended hours (9am-6pm weekdays) for accessibility
Emphasis on client communication and straightforward legal advice
cons: Website contains placeholder statistics ("0+") for years of experience and case numbers, raising questions about actual firm history
No individual attorney credentials, bar admission dates, or specific qualifications listed for named attorney
No verifiable case results, success rates, or client testimonials provided
Claims to be "largest consumer bankruptcy filing firm in Florida" without supporting evidence or rankings
Multiple phone numbers listed (305-267-3376 and 305-445-4855) with no explanation of discrepancy
best_for: Miami-Dade and Broward County residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing
Spanish-speaking consumers who prefer legal representation in their native language
Homeowners seeking foreclosure defense or mortgage modification assistance
Individuals needing bundled services combining bankruptcy with family law matters
diagnosis: Best suited for Miami-area residents needing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing who value Spanish-language capabilities and want bundled legal services. Primary caveat: Verify current attorney credentials and request specific case references, as the website lacks substantiating documentation for claimed experience levels and firm size claims.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
